scenario: After sentiment voting icons were upgraded from emojis (🔒📦⚖️🚜🚛) to Lucide SVG icons (Lock, Warehouse, Scale, Truck, Rocket), CLAUDE.md still referenced the old emoji icons in the Sentiment voting section. The documentation-agent should automatically detect when code changes invalidate existing documentation and flag or fix stale references.
expected_findings: Agent does not cross-reference code changes against CLAUDE.md content after implementation
No grep-based staleness detection step: search CLAUDE.md for literals that were just removed from code
Documentation updates are treated as optional cleanup rather than mandatory Gate 4
expected_patch_focus: Add post-implementation step: grep CLAUDE.md for any literals, names, or patterns that were just changed in code
Make documentation staleness detection part of the mandatory Gate 4 checklist
Require documentation-agent to verify CLAUDE.md accuracy after any UI component or API surface changes

replay_plan.failed_slice: Rename a component or change a constant referenced in CLAUDE.md — verify agent catches the stale reference
replay_plan.near_neighbor: Remove an RPC function — verify agent removes the corresponding monitoring query from CLAUDE.md
replay_plan.canary: Fix a CSS class typo in a component — should not trigger CLAUDE.md review (no documentation references)
